patterns.description_en: The opponent uses absolute words — finding one counter-example is enough to break them.
patterns.description_zh: 对手用绝对词，只要找到一个反例就破。
patterns.counter_en: That's not always true. For example, ___.

signals: lots of claims but no concrete example
patterns.description_en: Lots of claims but zero specific examples — call it out directly.
patterns.description_zh: 对手只有结论没有例子，直接质疑。
patterns.counter_en: Can my opponent give a real example?
patterns.counter_zh: 请问对手能举一个真实的例子吗？

signals: you're wrong because of who you are
patterns.description_en: The opponent attacks you personally instead of your argument.
patterns.description_zh: 对手攻击你这个人而不是你的论点。
patterns.counter_en: Let's focus on the topic, not on the person.
patterns.counter_zh: 我们应该关注辩题，不是个人。

signals: X happens, so Y must be the cause
patterns.description_en: The opponent treats a result as if it were the cause — a leap in logic.
patterns.description_zh: 对手把结果当成原因，跳跃推理。
patterns.counter_en: That's a result, not a reason.

patterns.description_en: The opponent dodges to an unrelated topic.
patterns.description_zh: 对手答非所问，扯到无关话题。
patterns.counter_en: That's a different topic. Let's go back to ___.
patterns.counter_zh: 那是另一个话题。让我们回到___。

questions.opponent_says: Everyone knows homework is bad. No kid likes doing homework.
questions.explanation_en: Used 'Everyone' and 'No kid' — absolute words. Just name one kid who likes homework.
questions.explanation_zh: 用了 'Everyone' 和 'No kid'，绝对化。可以举一个喜欢做作业的孩子作反例。

questions.opponent_says: Phones in school always cause problems. They never help.
questions.explanation_en: Both 'always' and 'never' are absolute words. Give one example of a phone helping learning.
questions.explanation_zh: 'always' 和 'never' 都是绝对词。可以举一个手机帮助学习的例子。

questions.opponent_says: School uniforms are bad because they look ugly and limit freedom and stop creativity.
questions.explanation_en: Three big claims, zero concrete examples.
questions.explanation_zh: 讲了三个结论，但没有给任何具体例子。

questions.opponent_says: You're only nine years old. You don't understand how the world works.
questions.explanation_en: Attacking your age, not discussing the actual motion.
questions.explanation_zh: 对手攻击你的年龄，没讨论辩题本身。

questions.opponent_says: Many kids hate homework. So homework must be making them unhappy.
questions.explanation_en: Treats 'kids hate it' as the cause of 'homework makes them sad'. Maybe homework is too hard — that's the real cause.
questions.explanation_zh: 把'孩子不喜欢'当成'作业让他们不开心'的原因。也许是因为作业太难，不是作业本身。

questions.opponent_says: We're talking about pets in school, but actually let me tell you about my summer vacation.
questions.explanation_en: Clearly went off topic.
questions.explanation_zh: 明显跑题了。

questions.opponent_says: All zoos are cruel. Every animal in a zoo is suffering.
questions.explanation_en: 'All' and 'every' are absolute words.
questions.explanation_zh: 'All' 和 'every' 是绝对词。

questions.opponent_says: Pocket money is good. It helps kids. It teaches them things.
questions.explanation_en: Many conclusions but zero concrete examples.
questions.explanation_zh: 结论很多但没有任何具体例子。

questions.opponent_says: Online learning is better. Just look — it exists, doesn't it?
questions.explanation_en: Doesn't actually answer 'why is it better' — skipped the argument entirely.
questions.explanation_zh: 没有真正回答 "为什么更好"，跳过了论证。

questions.opponent_says: Plastic bags are everywhere on beaches. So plastic bags must be the only reason for ocean pollution.
questions.explanation_en: Sees an effect (bags on beaches) and infers it's the only cause. Ocean pollution has many causes.
questions.explanation_zh: 看到结果（海滩有塑料袋）就推断成唯一原因。其实海洋污染原因很多。

questions.opponent_says: You're a kid, you don't understand school policy.
questions.explanation_en: Attacks you for being a kid instead of discussing the policy.
questions.explanation_zh: 攻击你是孩子，没讨论政策本身。

questions.opponent_says: TV is bad for everyone. No one benefits from it.
questions.explanation_en: 'everyone' and 'no one' are absolute words. Mention a science show that teaches kids.
questions.explanation_zh: 'everyone' 和 'no one' 是绝对词。可举科普节目帮助学习的例子。

questions.opponent_says: Country life is better. The air is fresh and people are nice.
questions.explanation_en: Talks about air and people, but no specifics (where, who, what happened).
questions.explanation_zh: 讲了空气和人，但没具体例子（哪里、谁、什么事）。

questions.opponent_says: Phones distract students. So banning phones will make kids learn more.
questions.explanation_en: Assumes phones are the only cause of bad learning. There are many reasons kids struggle.
questions.explanation_zh: 假设手机分散注意力是学习不好的唯一原因，但学习不好原因很多。

questions.opponent_says: I think kids should have homework, but speaking of which, what's your favorite cartoon?
questions.explanation_en: Classic topic switch.
questions.explanation_zh: 典型偷换话题。

questions.opponent_says: Everyone in our class wants school uniforms.
questions.explanation_en: 'Everyone' is an absolute word. One classmate who doesn't want uniforms breaks it.
questions.explanation_zh: 'Everyone' 是绝对词。一个不想穿的同学就能反驳。

questions.opponent_says: Pets are good for families. They make life better. Trust me.
questions.explanation_en: No examples, no reasoning at all.
questions.explanation_zh: 完全没有例子和理由。

questions.opponent_says: You can't argue with me, you're just a third grader.
questions.explanation_en: Attacks your grade level instead of discussing the motion.
questions.explanation_zh: 攻击年级，不讨论辩题。

questions.opponent_says: Many people throw away plastic bags. So plastic bags are the cause of all environmental problems.
questions.explanation_en: Sees one phenomenon and jumps to an exaggerated cause.
questions.explanation_zh: 看到现象就推一个夸大的因果结论。

questions.opponent_says: We're debating homework, but did you know my dog can do tricks?
questions.explanation_en: Off topic.
questions.explanation_zh: 跑题。

questions.opponent_says: All online classes are boring. Every kid hates them.
questions.explanation_en: 'All' and 'Every' — absolute words.
questions.explanation_zh: 'All' 和 'Every'，绝对化。

questions.opponent_says: Pocket money is bad because it spoils kids and creates problems.
questions.explanation_en: Zero concrete examples — all vague claims.
questions.explanation_zh: 没有任何具体例子，全是空泛的结论。

questions.opponent_says: You're a child, you can't understand money matters.
questions.explanation_en: Attacks you for being a child.
questions.explanation_zh: 攻击你是孩子。

questions.opponent_says: Some students fail tests. So tests must be the reason students hate school.
questions.explanation_en: Failing tests → hating school: treats an effect as the cause. A logical leap.
questions.explanation_zh: 考试失败 → 讨厌学校：把结果当原因。

questions.opponent_says: Let's discuss the school uniform debate. Anyway, I had pizza for lunch yesterday.
questions.explanation_en: Clearly off topic.
questions.explanation_zh: 明显跑题。
